title: Node 24, TypeScript 7, and AWS SDK for JavaScript v3 · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A TypeScript pairing room incrementally builds AWS SDK v3 TypeScript client contract, validates one changed runtime input, and keeps earlier HTTP, accessibility, security, stream, AbortSignal, and resource-budget rules active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of AWS SDK v3 TypeScript client contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin Node 24.18.0, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 whose compiler reports 6.0.3, and @aws-sdk/client-s3, lib-storage, s3-request-presigner, and cloudfront-signer 3.1087.0 as one reviewed compatibility contract.
Construct S3Client from immutable process configuration, short-lived credential and region providers, one owned request handler, and explicit shutdown and test lifetimes.
Treat command inputs, optional output fields, enums, paginators, metadata, and streaming bodies as SDK transport shapes that still require runtime and business validation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Runtime Sdk Version
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 5420 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce pinned Node, TypeScript, AWS SDK v3, modular client, runtime validation, and service-error narrowing; retain keyboard and screen-reader progress, cancellation, error, and recovery evidence; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Runtime Sdk Version.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for pin node 24.18.0, the typescript 7.0.2 native cli, the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 whose compiler reports 6.0.3, and @aws-sdk/client-s3, lib-storage, s3-request-presigner, and cloudfront-signer 3.1087.0 as one reviewed compatibility contract. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Runtime Sdk Version, keep Runtime Sdk Version connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.
